api/client/javascript/src.purpose: Public TypeScript SDK surface for OpenMeter, organised as four independent sub-packages by concern: the generated admin client (client/), the restricted portal-token client (portal/), the React integration (react/), and orval-generated Zod validation schemas (zod/). No source files live here directly — it is a routing layer; the generated halves (client/schemas.ts, zod/index.ts) are read-only.

patterns.name: Generated-first, never hand-edited
patterns.description: client/schemas.ts (openapi-fetch types) and zod/index.ts (orval schemas) are fully generated from the OpenAPI spec; any manual edit is overwritten by make gen-api.

patterns.name: Separate admin vs portal client (no merge)
patterns.description: client/ is the full admin SDK; portal/ is a portalToken-only client that intentionally omits all admin endpoints — capability separation is enforced at the type level, not by config.
patterns.example: // portal/index.ts: class OpenMeter { constructor({ portalToken }: Config) }

patterns.name: transformResponse wraps every fetch call
patterns.description: Every public method pipes the raw openapi-fetch result through transformResponse (date ISO→Date decoding + HTTPError throwing); portal/ reuses the same util from ../client/utils.js rather than redefining it.

patterns.name: RequestOptions threaded through every public method
patterns.description: Each method accepts options?: RequestOptions and spreads ...options into the fetch call to propagate AbortSignal.

patterns.name: React integration is a context/provider/hook trio delegating to portal/
patterns.description: react/ exposes exactly one OpenMeterContext, OpenMeterProvider, and useOpenMeter; all SDK logic lives in portal/ and react/ only handles injection.

key_file_guides.role: Root OpenMeter admin class instantiating every resource class as a public field.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new resource class must be registered here as a field + constructor new, or it is unreachable from the public API.

key_file_guides.role: Generated TS types for all OpenAPI paths/operations — source of truth for request/response shapes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never edit manually; import all domain types from here, never redefine locally.

key_file_guides.role: Shared transformResponse, encodeDates, and query date serialization.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Portal client imports these too — any signature change breaks both clients.

key_file_guides.role: Restricted portal client authenticating via portalToken, exposing only the portal meter query endpoint.
key_file_guides.watch_for: portalToken must never be optional; do not add admin endpoints; keep Accept: application/json on the meter query or it falls back to CSV.

key_file_guides.role: React context/provider/hook injecting the portal OpenMeter client; carries the 'use client' directive.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Context default is undefined (throws in hook) while null is the valid not-logged-in state — do not collapse the distinction; removing 'use client' breaks Next.js App Router.

key_file_guides.role: orval-generated Zod runtime-validation schemas over the TS types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never hand-edit; treat exported default/regex/min-max constants as regeneration-prone schema internals, not application constants.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing client/schemas.ts or zod/index.ts — both are generated and overwritten by make gen-api
Adding admin-level endpoints to portal/ — it is intentionally restricted to portal scope
Redefining transformResponse/encodeDates inside portal/ or react/ instead of importing from ../client/utils.js
Adding a client/ resource class without registering it as a public field on the root OpenMeter class
Removing the 'use client' directive from react/context.tsx — breaks Next.js App Router server components

decisions.decision: Four sub-packages (client, portal, react, zod) instead of one entry point
decisions.rationale: Separates generated types from hand-authored utilities, admin from portal access, and framework-agnostic client from React-specific integration.

decisions.decision: Portal client is a separate class, not a config mode of the admin client
decisions.rationale: Enforces at the type level that portal consumers cannot call admin endpoints, avoiding accidental capability escalation.

decisions.decision: Zod schemas generated by orval from the OpenAPI spec
decisions.rationale: Keeps runtime validation in sync with the TypeSpec-derived spec; any spec change propagates via make gen-api with no manual maintenance.
